top_signals.title: Iran rejects France-Oman demining plan for Strait of Hormuz
top_signals.summary: Oil prices have gained after Iran rejected any third-party interference on clearing mines laid in the Strait of Hormuz. Earlier this week, French President Emmanuel Macron proposed a collaboration between France and Oman with international partners on demining the Strait of Hormuz. The announcement came after the International Maritime Organization (IMO) suspended the planned evacuation ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: The Tech Download: Amazon’s devices chief Panos Panay on tech giant's AI gadget push
top_signals.summary: CNBC's Arjun Kharpal sits down Amazon's Panay on the latest episode of The Tech Download podcast.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: OpenAI proposes 5% stake to Trump administration to ease Washington pressure: Report
top_signals.summary: Trump said in June that the U.S. taking an ownership stake in AI giants would be "a beautiful thing" and make American public "partners in this revolution."
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: THINK Ahead: The calm before the CPI
top_signals.summary: THINK Ahead in developed markets United States (James Knightley) It is a quiet week for US data, sandwiched between a softer-than-expected US jobs report and a June CPI release scheduled for 14 July, which should show prices at the headline level falling month-on-month due to the plunge in gasoline prices. This may push the market ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: US fuel supply trends higher in June
top_signals.summary: The US has supplied 25% more fuel oil per day on average in June compared to May, according to the Energy Information Administration (EIA). Changes in monthly US residual fuel oil from May to June: Production up 22,000 b/d to 303,000 b/d Supply up 70,000 b/d to 353,000 b/d Imports steady at 112,000 b/d Volumes of ...
